name: Meningioma
subtitle: Usually starts in the lining around the brain or spinal cord
summary: Meningiomas are among the most common primary brain tumors. Many are slow-growing, though higher-grade meningiomas can behave more aggressively.
symptoms: Headaches, seizures, weakness, vision changes, or no symptoms if found incidentally.
diagnosis: MRI is commonly used first. If surgery is done, pathology helps confirm the exact grade.
treatment: Observation for some tumors, surgery, focused radiation, or radiation after surgery when needed.
questions: Is this tumor pressing on nearby brain tissue? What grade is it? Is monitoring safe, or is treatment recommended now?
